title: ulmus glabra f. concavifolia
intro: Ulmus glabra f. concavifolia, also known as the Wych Elm or English Elm, is a deciduous tree species that belongs to the family Ulmaceae. Its bark, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Ulmus glabra f. concavifolia include treating fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as providing relief from respiratory problems and wounds. Herbal preparations of Ulmus glabra f. concavifolia can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
